free_text: Elastic and inelastic scattering of 17.5 and 40.0 MeV
3He particles from 11B.
Variable Energy Cyclotron was used for
40.0 MeV measurements.
Tandem Van de Graaff Generator was used
for 17.5 MeV measurements.
3He beam.
In the 40 MeV measurements the beam was up to 10 mu-A.
360 mu-g/cm2 self-supporting 11B foil target was placed
at the centre of a 66 cm diameter scattering chamber in
the 40.0 MeV measurements.
In the 17.5 MeV measurements the target was a 92
mu-g/cm2 thick, self-supporting foil.
Two Delta-E - E telescopes was placed about
20 cm from the target in the 40 MeV measurements.
24-channel Browne-Buechner type multigap
spectrograph was used for the 17.5 MeV measurements.
Nuclear emulsion plates were used for the
17.5 MeV measurements.
Error from the graphic bars. No source of
uncertainty is given.
In the 40 MeV measurements the overall error for the
elastic and forward angle inelastic data were about 5%.
At backward angles the total uncertainty never exceeded
10%. In the 17.5 MeV measurements the overall error
varied from about 6% for the elastic and forward angle
inelastic measurements and approximately 10 % for the
backward angle inelastic cross sections.
ERR-ANALYS was changed. Sub 003 modified.
Fig.3
Sub 003 was split into two subentries.
